tree.setup: A family restaurant where the tables have glass tops over floral tablecloths. Grandma Li runs the front while her daughter cooks. A cat sleeps on a chair by the door as if it owns the place. The menu is handwritten on a whiteboard, and Grandma Li has crossed out two items with no explanation.
tree.setup_zh: 一家家庭餐馆，桌子上铺着花布，盖着玻璃。李奶奶管前台，她女儿做菜。一只猫在门口的椅子上睡觉，好像这是它的店。菜单写在白板上，李奶奶划掉了两道菜，没有解释。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_pinyin: lái la! xiǎng chī diǎn shénme?
turns.text_en: You're here! What would you like to eat?

options.feedback: Grandma Li beams. She loves this question. She leans in conspiratorially:「今天的鱼很好。」今天 (jīntiān) + adjective is a natural way to ask for daily recommendations.

options.text_pinyin: nǐ hǎo, wǒ yào chī fàn.
options.feedback: Grandma Li chuckles -- well, yes, that's why you're here. 吃饭 literally means 'eat rice' but it's the general word for having a meal.
You point at something on the whiteboard. Grandma Li squints at her own handwriting.「哦，那个啊……」Using 那个 to point things out is perfectly natural.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你要不要一个凉菜？很好吃的！
turns.text_pinyin: nǐ yào bú yào yí gè liángcài? hěn hǎochī de!
turns.text_en: Do you want a cold dish? It's very tasty!

options.text_pinyin: hǎo de, wǒ yào yí gè.
options.feedback: Grandma Li nods with satisfaction. She was going to bring it anyway, but she appreciates the formality of being asked.
Grandma Li explains with hand gestures: cold, vegetables, sauce. She seems genuinely delighted to teach you. 凉菜 are chilled appetizer dishes -- a staple of Chinese dining.
Grandma Li looks at you the way a mother looks at a child who refuses vegetables. She'll respect your choice. Barely.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_en: OK! It'll be ready in a moment.

tree.cultural_note: 凉菜 (liángcài, cold dishes) are served at the start of a meal -- cucumber salad, spiced tofu skin, cold noodles. They're not leftovers; they're intentionally chilled. First-time visitors sometimes expect hot food and are surprised!
